title: Cannot start a container imported from a newer version of podman.ssh
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

An image created and saved on podman 2.1.1 (from Fedora 33) that is imported into podman 1.6.4 (to CentOS 8.2.2004) is failing to run. The error is:
`Error: container_linux.go:349: starting container process caused "exec: \"/bin/sh\": stat /bin/sh: no such file or directory": OCI runtime command not found error`

- I am running the containers _rootless_ on both the source and targets 
- Using `podman run -it repository/imagename:tag` works on the source.
- Using `podman start` and then `podman attach` for the built container also works on the source.


**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Build container on podman 2.1.1 `podman build ./path/to/dockerfile_directory/ -t repository/imagename:tag`

2. Save container to tarball `podman save > image-name.tar repository/imagename:tag` 

3. Copy to remote system with podman 1.6.4 (CentOS 8)

4. Import the tarball to the local repository `cat image-name.tar | podman import -`

5. Attempt to run the image either via CMD or setting a new ENTRYPOINT `podman run -it repository/imagename:tag /bin/sh` **or** `podman run -it -entrypoint "/bin/sh" repository/imagename:tag`

**Describe the results you received:**
The target site produces the following error:
`Error: container_linux.go:349: starting container process caused "exec: \"/bin/sh\": stat /bin/sh: no such file or directory": OCI runtime command not found error`


**Describe the results you expected:**
Expected the target site to start the container process normally via both entrypoint and command.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**
I've exported and imported from podman 1.6.4 to podman 1.6.4, this appears to work as expected.

**Output of `podman version`:**

- **Podman Source (Build/Save):**
```
Version:      2.1.1
API Version:  2.0.0
Go Version:   go1.15.2
Built:        Wed Oct  7 09:21:20 2020
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

- **Podman Target (Import/Run):**
```
Version:            1.6.4
RemoteAPI Version:  1
Go Version:         go1.13.4
OS/Arch:            linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

- **Podman Source (Build/Save):**

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

- **Podman Source (Build/Save):**
```
podman-2.1.1-12.fc33.x86_64
```

- **Podman Target #1(Import/Run):**
```
podman-1.6.4-10.module_el8.2.0+305+5e198a41.x86_64
```

- **Podman Target #2(Import/Run):**
```
podman-1.6.4-10.module_el8.2.0+305+5e198a41.x86_64
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide?**

- Latest Version: NO
- Troubleshooting Guide: YES

Issue 26 was the closest to the error, however architecture on both all systems involved in tests is x86_64.

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
- Fedora 33: Physical host
- CentOS 8: Virtual Machine (Podman Target #1) & Physical (Podman Target #2)

Both physical/virtual targets are same OS and kept in sync. Most of the requested details I did not provide a unique output for both, so all results are Target #1.  I did include both results for the package version to provide confirmation the same RPM was in use in physical and virtual CentOS targets.

Thanks

comments.body:
I think some commands were mixed up.

`podman save/load` are for images while `podman export/import` are for containers.

> I think some commands were mixed up.
> 
> `podman save/load` are for images while `podman export/import` are for containers.

The issue definitely is on my side with running the wrong command. The above statement makes it clear and we can close the ticket.

**Food for thought**

Since one is about images and the other about containers I find it interesting that the word container is found in podman save documentation for **container archive** and podman load has it multiple times referencing **container image archive** or the prior **container archive**. Yet the podman import documentation never once uses the word container or references creating a **container image archive**. Import doc however does clearly list podman-export under the **see-also** section. 

I'm not requesting any specific doc changes, just providing some thoughts. 

Thanks
Looks like @QiWang19 already opened a PR (https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/8459), thanks!

Great. If someone else has taken up the torch I'll leave it to them.
Otherwise I'm definitely willing to think of revisions and submit PR's for
documentation.

Thanks!
